stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced YESTERDAY (Apr 22, 2026): revenue Rs 2,065 cr (+20% YoY), PAT Rs 842 cr (+31% YoY), operating income Rs 1,049 cr (+39%), operating margin 51%. Board declared second interim dividend of Rs 270/share; record date May 7, 2026, payment on/before May 21, 2026. FY26 consolidated revenue Rs 7,672 cr (+12%), PAT Rs 2,639 cr (+11%). Remaining performance obligations Rs 7,761 cr indicate strong deal pipeline.
stocks.sector_context: Indian IT Q4 season muted - TCS in-line, Wipro/HCL Tech missed with weak FY27 guidance (HCL 1-4% CC, Wipro Q1 -2% to 0%). Infosys reports today (Apr 23). US Apr-2 reciprocal tariffs + GenAI deflation weighing on sector sentiment. OFSS's banking-software niche is relatively insulated from discretionary-IT-spend slowdown.
stocks.peer_comparison: OFSS Q4 print is the standout beat of the IT season - 51% operating margin is best-in-class vs large-cap peers (~21-25% EBIT). Revenue +20% YoY dwarfs TCS/Wipro/HCL mid-single-digit growth. However stock has been a laggard - down ~40% over last 12 months despite strong fundamentals, suggesting de-rating on ownership concerns.
red_flags: Stock down ~40% in last 1 year despite strong fundamentals
Parent Oracle Corp ownership concentration - limited free float, low liquidity
Broader IT sector FII outflow pressure from US tariff theme
Sales CAGR only 7% over 5 years - growth reacceleration needs to sustain
catalysts: Q4 beat + Rs 270/share dividend announced Apr 22 - potential re-rating today
Dividend record date May 7, 2026 (ex-date buying)
Strong RPO Rs 7,761 cr gives FY27 revenue visibility
Infosys Q4 today may set broader IT-sector tone

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results TODAY (Apr 23, 2026) - announcement post-market ~3:45pm IST, call 5:30pm IST. Consensus: Revenue ~Rs 46,300-46,738 cr (+13.7% YoY), PAT ~Rs 7,500-7,790 cr (+4-9% YoY), EBIT margin ~21.4%. Board to consider final FY26 dividend. Stock reaction: dropped 5.2% on results-day amid cautious FY27 guidance of 2-5% CC revenue growth (vs ~4.2% delivered in FY26).
stocks.sector_context: IT sector Q4 season has been weak - Wipro guided -2% to 0% for Q1, HCL Tech FY27 guidance 1-4% CC disappointed. Infosys' 2-5% FY27 guide is also below street hopes. Drivers: US tariff-related client indecision, GenAI deflationary pricing pressure, cautious discretionary spend. Nifty IT index under pressure through April.
stocks.peer_comparison: Infosys FY27 guidance (2-5% CC) is marginally better than HCL (1-4%) and Wipro Q1 (-2% to 0%), in line with sector. Margin guide 20-22% EBIT is stable. Infosys typically commands premium valuation vs peers, so even in-line results with cautious guidance drive multiple compression - today's 5.2% drop reflects this.
red_flags: Stock dropped 5.2% on Apr 23, 2026 post-results (per wire reports)
FY27 revenue guidance 2-5% CC signals growth deceleration vs FY26
GenAI deflationary impact flagged by management as structural concern
US tariff-driven client indecision cited on deal ramp-ups
catalysts: Final FY26 dividend announcement today
Topaz GenAI deal mix commentary may reset narrative
Management call at 5:30pm IST - margin/deal-book tone is key
Potential stock stabilization after capitulation if guide is digested

stocks.recent_news: Filed amalgamation application with NCLT Mumbai on Apr 18, 2026 (three WOS: Piramal Corporate Tower, Piramal Agastya Offices, DHFL Investments). Share price Rs 1,721.5 on Apr 16, 2026; market cap Rs 39,256 cr. Board approved Rs 15,000 cr NCD issuance in tranches from Apr 1, 2026 to Mar 31, 2027. Q2 FY26 PAT Rs 326.83 cr (+100.55% YoY). Direct-listed Nov 7, 2025 via PEL merger (no IPO).
stocks.sector_context: NBFC sector under RBI regulatory recalibration; peers Bajaj Finance reports Q4 on Apr 29. Upper-layer NBFC classification evolving - Piramal Finance's scale puts it in scope. Interest-rate trajectory supportive but credit-cost discipline is in focus post DHFL retail-book integration.
stocks.peer_comparison: Piramal Finance trails Bajaj Finance and Shriram Finance on ROE/scale - still a turnaround story post-DHFL. The 100% YoY PAT growth is high but off a low base. Stock has been range-bound Rs 1,700-1,860 through April 2026 while Bajaj Finance has traded firm into its Apr-29 print.
red_flags: Rs 15,000 cr NCD issuance signals heavy near-term funding needs
Direct-listing (no IPO price discovery) means uncertain valuation floor
Independent Director Gautam Doshi resigned Mar 27, 2026
Upper-layer NBFC reclassification risk = stricter governance/capital norms
catalysts: FY26 results release post Rs 1 lakh cr AUM milestone
NCLT approval of amalgamation scheme filed Apr 18
Bajaj Finance Q4 on Apr 29 will set NBFC sector tone
Successive NCD tranches through FY27

stocks.recent_news: Sold entire stake in Svaas Wellness subsidiary to Enspirit Technology Services for Rs 2.23 cr on Apr 7, 2026. Board weighed final FY26 dividend Apr 16, 2026. Stock closed Rs 1,217.80 on Apr 15 (-1.46%). Launched Semaglutide injection 'Obeda' (first DCGI approved) for Type 2 diabetes in India. Brazil's ANVISA rejected DRREDDY's Embeltah (semaglutide+liraglutide) registration on Apr 13, 2026. Q4 FY26 results due May 12, 2026.
stocks.sector_context: US tariffs announced Apr 2, 2026 - 100% on patented drugs, Indian generics exempt for now (life-saving drugs at 0% duty). Commerce Secretary to report on generic action within 1 year. Nifty Pharma under pressure through April; Indian pharma export value $10.5B to US in FY25 at stake. DRREDDY specialty + generics mix gives partial shield but branded exposure is a concern.
stocks.peer_comparison: DRREDDY underperforming: -2% YTD 2026, -5.7% 1M, -10% 1Y. Aurobindo (+14% YTD) and Cipla (domestic-heavy) both outperforming. Analyst 12M targets Rs 1,500-1,600 imply upside but require tariff resolution. Trading ~15% below 52-week high Rs 1,480.
red_flags: Brazil ANVISA rejection on Embeltah (semaglutide+liraglutide) Apr 13, 2026
Stock -5.7% in last month, -10% over 1 year
High US revenue exposure under tariff uncertainty
Trading ~15% below 52-week high without clear catalyst
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results May 12, 2026
Final FY26 dividend decision at Apr-16 board meeting
Obeda (semaglutide) domestic launch revenue ramp
Potential US tariff carve-out extension for generics

stocks.recent_news: Rs 800 cr share buyback tender at Rs 1,475/share (up to 54.24 lakh shares) - record date was Apr 12, 2026. Board approved buyback plan Apr 6. Secured USFDA final approvals Apr 2026: Dapagliflozin+Metformin HCl ER (US$514M segment, 180-day shared exclusivity) and Dapagliflozin 5/10 mg (Farxiga generic, US$10.2B segment, 180-day shared exclusivity). Analyst fair value reset to ~Rs 1,379.96 (from Rs 1,366.07).
stocks.sector_context: Aurobindo is outperforming a weak Nifty Pharma - stock up ~14% YTD 2026 vs sector/benchmark down. US tariff theme (100% on patented drugs) actually benefits pure-generics players like Aurobindo that aren't on the patented-drug list. FDA approval pace (Dapagliflozin combos + single-agent) is a competitive advantage.
stocks.peer_comparison: Clear outperformer - Aurobindo +14% YTD vs DRREDDY -2% YTD, Cipla roughly flat. Buyback at Rs 1,475 acts as soft floor. Generics-heavy mix plus fresh high-value FDA wins (Dapagliflozin is the biggest Farxiga-generic launch opportunity of CY2026) gives earnings visibility peers lack.
red_flags: Shared 180-day exclusivity on Dapagliflozin splits revenue with other filers
Post-buyback mean-reversion risk once tender closes
Execution risk on simultaneous launch of 3-4 new products
Buyback price Rs 1,475 could cap upside in near term as arbs sell
catalysts: Buyback tender supportive technical floor
Immediate Dapagliflozin (Farxiga generic) US launch - $10.2B addressable segment
Dapagliflozin+Metformin ER combo launch
Q4 FY26 results expected in May 2026
